behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is at the module boundary — missing or malformed data when modules hand off to each other. Unit tests with individually-mocked sources would pass even if the aggregation doesn't correctly combine field names or structure from actual API responses. Real failure only appears when the three collection modules are wired together and their outputs must match the summary function's expected input schema.
behaviors.test_description: Create realistic mock responses from the three data sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker, deployment log), call the aggregation pipeline, and verify the payload passed to summary-generation contains all three sources' data with correct field names, data types, and no missing fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is the orchestration layer's ability to coordinate when a dependent module fails. Unit tests with mocks that never fail would miss real integration bugs: async timing issues, partial-response handling, or recovery-path logic that only manifests when one real module fails while others succeed. The failure boundary is at module interaction, not within a single function.
behaviors.test_description: Mock one data source to fail (e.g., timeout, HTTP 500) while others succeed, execute the collection and aggregation pipeline, and verify the output includes a 'data unavailable' note for the failed source and complete, correct data for the others.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: If the summary generator is template-based or algorithmic (deterministic), the failure is within one function and Unit is sufficient. However, if it uses an LLM, correctness depends on real model output and Agentic is required instead. The behavior's emphasis on 'accurately represents' suggests quality judgment, which for an LLM is non-deterministic — the same input may produce different summaries across runs. Unit tests with a mocked LLM would always pass, hiding real quality failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create test data with known key values (e.g., '5 deployments, 99.5% uptime, 3 incidents'), call the summary function, and verify the output accurately includes and represents those values (matching numbers, correct terminology, complete coverage of all sources).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assumes template-based summary generation. If the summary generator is LLM-based, this behavior requires Agentic-level testing with the real model to catch quality failures that mocks would hide.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: End-to-end pipeline test requiring all modules to be wired and coordinated together. Unit tests of individual components (data collection, summary, email sending) would pass even if the integration fails (e.g., data format mismatch between modules, missing fields in email payload). Real failures appear only when the full pipeline runs together. System level only needed if testing requires real external service calls (actual email delivery to real inbox).
behaviors.test_description: Mock the three data sources and email service, execute the complete pipeline from data collection through email composition and sending, and verify an email with the complete, correctly-formatted report is sent to the configured recipient list.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If summary generation is LLM-based, may need Agentic-level testing for output quality. If email delivery must succeed against real email infrastructure (not mocked), elevate to System.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode spans multiple modules: error detection, file I/O, and scheduler coordination. Unit tests of just the 'decide to save and retry' logic would miss real failures: the report file might not persist correctly, the scheduler might not pick it up on the next run, or the retry flow might fail due to missing file path or permissions. Real failure only appears when the error-handling flow is tested end-to-end across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the email service to fail, execute the pipeline to trigger the fallback, verify the report is written to the expected file path with correct content, then simulate the next scheduled run and verify delivery is retried from the saved file.
